name: Status Update
summary: Concise progress-report arc for keeping a stakeholder informed: headline, brief, progress, results, plan, feedback. Optimized for short, repeated updates rather than one-time deep dives.

beats.name: Headline
beats.description: Open with the single most important sentence the audience needs to know — on track, off track, or shifted. The headline replaces 'how's it going' with a clear answer.

beats.name: Brief
beats.description: State the objective for the period and what was promised. One slide, three lines. Frames the rest of the update so progress is read against a clear bar.

beats.name: Progress
beats.description: Walk through the actions taken since the last update. Specific, attributable, dated. Avoid effort theater — list outcomes and material moves, not activity for its own sake.

beats.name: Results
beats.description: Show the outcomes those actions produced. Metrics, customer signals, decisions made. Keep the comparison crisp — versus plan, versus last period, versus benchmark.

beats.name: Action Plan
beats.description: Outline the next steps and who owns them. Be specific about the next decision point and what would have to happen to slip the schedule.

beats.name: Feedback
beats.description: Invite the audience to weigh in: where you'd like input, where you need a decision, where help would unblock you. End the update with an open hand, not a closed file.
